_comment_for_translators: This file holds engine-level UI chrome only — labels that exist regardless of which target language is active. Per-language strings (lesson titles, hero/footer text, vocab labels, inline phrase glosses) live in src/languages/<code>/locales/.
test_helper: Try translating, then click to reveal.
picker_title: What do you want to speak?
picker_subtitle: Pick a language. Five minutes from now you'll have said your first real sentence — past, present, or future.
picker_footer: Each language has its own complete guide — vocabulary, dialogues, drills, and a 32-day study plan.
onboarding_step1_title: You already understand more than you think.
onboarding_step1_body: Five minutes from now, you will have spoken a real sentence aloud — in the past, present, or future, your choice.
onboarding_step1_aside: No grammar lecture. No vocabulary list. Just the structural skeleton — three tenses, three forms, any verb. Master that and you can already say almost anything.
onboarding_step2_subtitle: This is a Romance language, full of words nearly identical to English. You just stole twenty-four of them, free.
onboarding_step3_title: One verb. Three tenses. Already a system.
onboarding_step3_aside: Notice the pattern. The structure stays the same — only the markers change.
onboarding_step4_prompt: How would you say "{{en}}" — present tense, first person?
onboarding_step5_title: You just generated a real sentence.
onboarding_step5_body: Three tenses. Three forms. Six subjects. Any verb.
onboarding_step5_aside: The textbook ahead of you teaches the system. Lesson 1 — The Matrix — is the place to look first. Five minutes a day for thirty days. By Day 30 you can ask, state, or deny anything in past, present, or future.
